title: Drip vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Campaign Monitor: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your business best.
h1: Drip vs Campaign Monitor: Complete Comparison for 2025
intro: Drip and Campaign Monitor are two established email marketing platforms with different strengths. Drip excels at e-commerce automation and store integration, while Campaign Monitor focuses on beautiful design and multi-brand management. Choosing between them depends on your business type and specific feature needs.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce businesses with deep store integration
comparison_table.tool_b: Agencies and teams managing multiple brands

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, visual automation, SMS
comparison_table.tool_b: Template design, multi-brand management, journey designer, transactional email

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and need deep payment processor integration and revenue tracking
You want SMS marketing built into your email platform
You need advanced automation with visual workflow builders for complex customer journeys
choose_b_if: You're looking for the lowest starting price and want to minimize initial investment
You manage multiple client accounts or brands under one dashboard
You prioritize beautiful email templates and design-focused features over e-commerce functionality

faq.q: Is Drip better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. Drip is better for e-commerce businesses needing advanced automation and revenue tracking, earning a higher G2 rating (4.4 vs 4.1). Campaign Monitor excels for agencies managing multiple brands with lower pricing and superior design tools. Neither is universally 'better'—it's about fit.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor is significantly cheaper, starting at $11/month compared to Drip's $39/month. This makes Campaign Monitor the better choice for budget-conscious businesses or those just starting email marketing. However, Drip's higher price reflects its e-commerce-specific features.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms typically support importing contact lists and email templates. The migration process is relatively straightforward, though you may need to recreate automation workflows since each platform has different automation builders. Most users report the switch takes a few hours to a day depending on complexity.
